references.title: Analytics of Learning Strategies: the Association with the Personality Traits

extracted.Courses: 1 Sit Less, Get Active Online Course
extracted.Data sources:
LMS logs, 
Questionnaires for Personality Traits
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Learning actions were grouped in learning sessions and these were given to a first-order Markov Model, which subsequently were sued as inputs to an EM model.

Authors define 30 minutes as the threshold that separates 2 learning sessions.
extracted.Cluster Description:
Long Diverse: most common, focus on information and video.

Video Oriented: Course videos.

Information Oriented: Main focus on is with supplementary learning materials → this tactic contains the smaller od actions per session.

Assessment Oriented: Moderate long sessions.
extracted.Implications:
Personality traits are predictive of choice of learning strategy.

However, providing assistance based on personality traits should only be a starting point.
extracted.Comments: Each cluster refers to the clustering of Learning Sessions into learning tactics.
extracted.Limitations:
Sampling Bias → most participants were female.

Use of self-reports nad limitations of the self-reports used.

extracted.Courses: 1 Sit Less, Get Active Online Course
extracted.Data sources:
LMS logs, 
Questionnaires for Personality Traits
extracted.Cluster Description:
Disengaged: Largest group and lowest level of activity. students exhibit the lowest course performance.

Surface: Moderate levels of activity, often especially focused on either assessments. In general they have relatively low marks in the course.

Active: 30%  of learners follow this approach. For the final course topic learners increased Information Oriented tactic.

Highly Active: High levels of activity starts using the Long _Diverse Oriented tactic but the tactic use decreases with time, wherein learners in this group also use Video Oriented Tactic. Learners in this strategy have the highet approval rate.
extracted.Implications:
Personality traits are predictive of choice of learning strategy.

However, providing assistance based on personality traits should only be a starting point.
extracted.Comments: Each cluster refers to learning tactics adopted by students throughout their learning sessions.
extracted.Limitations:
Sampling Bias → most participants were female.

Use of self-reports nad limitations of the self-reports used.

annotations.text: There is a research stream focusing on extracting learning strategies from multiple sources and multiple learning settings and checking how these different learning strategies map into overall performance. However, tow gaps are apparent: doing this for MOOCs is still relatively under-explored AND connections between uncovered strategies and psychological constructs is usually not performed.

The paper uses process and sequence-mining techniques to detect learning strategies from MOOC logs and tries to map them to the Big Five personality traits. Results show 4 distinct learning strategies that were predicted by conscientiousness, emotional instability and agreeableness.
